narrowing_rationale: Excluded T2 (PARTIALLY_EXPLORED, 11 cross-field papers — polymer brush analogy for glycocalyx is established). Excluded T4 (DISJOINT but bridge factually questionable — quantum topological protection has no classical biofilm analogue). Excluded T5 (PARTIALLY_EXPLORED, 4 papers on Turing instability in cancer). Retained T1, T3, T6 — all DISJOINT with validated bridges and diverse strategies (contradiction_mining, scale_bridging, structural_isomorphism).

scout_targets.field_a: Manganese speciation toxicology (Mn2+ neurotoxicity, SLC30A10 mutations, globus pallidus accumulation)
scout_targets.field_c: Deinococcus radiodurans Mn-antioxidant defense (Mn-OP complexes, DP1 decapeptide, radioresistance)
bridge_concepts: Free Mn2+ neurotoxicity vs Mn-orthophosphate/peptide complexes (Mn-OP) protective ROS scavenging
DP1 synthetic decapeptide converting toxic Mn2+ to protective Mn-antioxidant complex (>100-fold IC50 shift)
Irving-Williams series position enabling speciation-dependent toxicity/protection switching
MnSOD enzymatic vs Mn-OP non-enzymatic catalytic ROS scavenging — orthogonal mechanisms
SLC30A10 efflux transporter mutations — speciation at transporter interface unknown
scout_targets.why_disjoint: Radiation biology/extremophile community and manganese neurotoxicology have zero cross-citations. DP1/Mn-OP work focused on radiation protection, never applied to neurodegeneration.

scout_targets.field_a: Polymer brush physics (Alexander-de Gennes scaling, brush compression, osmotic pressure)
scout_targets.field_c: Glycocalyx-mediated endothelial mechanotransduction (syndecan-1, glypican-1, shear stress sensing)
bridge_concepts: Alexander-de Gennes polymer brush scaling laws applied to glycocalyx compression
Osmotic pressure in brush compression maps to glycocalyx shear stress sensing
HSPG chains as biological polymer brushes with measurable persistence length and grafting density
Brush collapse transition mirrors glycocalyx shedding in sepsis/diabetes
Stefan-Reynolds squeeze-film lubrication in polymer brushes maps to glycocalyx hydrodynamic boundary layer
scout_targets.why_disjoint: Polymer physicists study brush theory for synthetic surfaces. Vascular biologists study glycocalyx through biochemical frameworks. Scaling law formalism not applied to predict glycocalyx mechanotransduction quantitatively.

scout_targets.field_a: Classical nucleation theory (CNT, Volmer-Weber 1926, Becker-Doring 1935, free energy barriers)
scout_targets.field_c: Ferroptosis iron pool dynamics (labile iron pool, ferritin iron loading, iron-dependent cell death 2012)
bridge_concepts: CNT free energy barrier DeltaG* predicting ferrihydrite nucleation within ferritin nanocages during LIP overflow
Supersaturation ratio S = [Fe2+]/[Fe2+]_eq as quantitative predictor of nucleation rate
Ostwald ripening kinetics (LSW theory) predicting ferrihydrite core growth rate vs ferroptosis induction timescale
Ferritin cage geometry (8nm inner diameter) imposing maximum core size constraint absent in bulk CNT
Critical nucleus size r* predicting minimum iron atoms (~20 Fe) for stable ferrihydrite core
scout_targets.why_disjoint: CNT is materials science/physical chemistry (1926). Ferroptosis is cell biology (2012). Ferritin biochemists measure total iron loading but have not applied CNT formalism to nucleation kinetics within protein cages.

scout_targets.field_a: Topological insulator surface states (bulk-boundary correspondence, Chern numbers, protected edge states)
scout_targets.field_c: Bacterial biofilm electrical signaling (K+ channel waves, long-range communication, B. subtilis oscillations)
bridge_concepts: Topological protection of conducting edge states as framework for boundary-preferential biofilm signaling
Bulk-boundary correspondence: biofilm interior dormant (insulating) while boundary cells signal (conducting)
Winding number classification of biofilm signaling network topology
Robustness to disorder prediction: signals robust to ~30% random cell removal
Berry phase analogue in biofilm oscillatory signaling under nutrient gradient cycling
scout_targets.why_disjoint: Condensed matter physics and biofilm microbiology have zero shared literature. Biofilm signaling studied through ion channel biology and reaction-diffusion, never topological band theory.

scout_targets.field_a: Reaction-diffusion morphogenesis (Turing instability, pattern formation, activator-inhibitor dynamics)
scout_targets.field_c: Tumor mutational burden spatial heterogeneity (immune hot/cold zones, spatial transcriptomics, immunotherapy response)
bridge_concepts: Turing instability conditions applied to spatial patterning of tumor subclones via immune-growth factor diffusivity ratio
Predicted characteristic wavelength lambda measurable from spatial transcriptomics autocorrelation
2D vs 3D Turing pattern transitions predicting tumor morphology changes
Immune hot/cold zones as activator-high/inhibitor-high Turing pattern domains
Meinhardt activator-substrate depletion model for tumor necrotic core patterning
scout_targets.why_disjoint: Turing pattern community focuses on developmental biology. Tumor heterogeneity community uses evolutionary/ecological frameworks. No paper applies Turing instability to predict tumor mutational burden spatial wavelengths.

scout_targets.field_a: Granular jamming physics (jamming transition, force chains, Edwards entropy, dilatancy, Gardner transition)
scout_targets.field_c: Chromatin compaction phase transitions (euchromatin/heterochromatin, Hi-C domains, nucleosome packing)
bridge_concepts: Jamming phase diagram (T, 1/phi, sigma) maps to chromatin state space (histone modifications, nucleosome density, transcriptional forces)
Edwards entropy for mechanically stable configurations analogous to chromatin accessible configurations
Force chain networks in jammed packings as model for TAD stress transmission
Dilatancy prediction: transcription at compacted loci requires transient local chromatin expansion
Gardner transition predicts heterochromatin near jamming shows anomalous non-Gaussian fluctuation dynamics
scout_targets.why_disjoint: Granular physics and chromatin biology are separate communities. Polymer physics (unjammed) dominates chromatin modeling. Jamming perspective — where packing fraction controls a phase transition — is unexplored for chromatin.
